us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran as Deadline Looms and Talks Stall
us-politics.body:
President Trump held a press conference Sunday after posting a profanity-laced ultimatum demanding Iran open the Strait of Hormuz. Iran rejected the US proposal as 'unrealistic,' while Trump said Tehran had made a 'significant proposal' of its own - but called it 'not good enough.' Talks remain deadlocked.

Israel struck an Iranian petrochemical plant ahead of the US deadline, raising the stakes further. JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warned that a war with Iran could tip the US economy into recession. Supporters of Trump's hardline approach say maximum pressure is working; critics argue the brinkmanship risks a conflict with severe economic consequences.

sources.title: Trump set to hold press conference after profanity-laced post on Iran
Trump reveals Iran made 'significant proposal' after ultimatum, but 'not good enough'
Israel hits petrochemical plant in Iran ahead of Trump deadline
Live updates: Trump to hold press conferences as deadline nears for Iran to open Strait of Hormuz
Dimon warns Iran war could sink economy into a recession
Iran spurns ‘unrealistic’ US peace proposal
Watch live: Trump briefs press ahead of deadline on Iran

us-politics.headline: Supreme Court Clears Path to Dismiss Bannon Conviction
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court sided with Steve Bannon on Sunday, clearing the way for his contempt of Congress conviction to be dismissed. The ruling follows Trump's pardon of Bannon and applies similar logic to a separate case involving a pardoned former Cincinnati city councilman convicted of bribery.

Supporters say the decisions reflect the proper scope of presidential pardon power. Critics argue the rulings shield Trump allies from accountability for defying congressional oversight. The court also returned a separate state-secrets privilege case to a lower court, adding to a busy day of consequential rulings.

sources.title: Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in bid to dismiss Jan. 6 conviction
Supreme Court returns state-secrets privilege case to lower court
Supreme Court clears the way for Bannon contempt case to be dismissed
Supreme Court clears way for dismissal in case of ex-Cincinnati City Council member pardoned by Trump
Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon contempt of Congress case

us-politics.headline: Democrats Plot Comeback While Debating Their Own Weaknesses
us-politics.body:
Democrats announced their first slate of state legislative targets ahead of November's midterms, focusing on flipping Republican-held seats. Party strategists argue structural headwinds still favour Democrats, pointing to voter backlash against Trump-era policies.

But internal critics are less optimistic. Several analysts warn the party is too quick to celebrate Trump's stumbles rather than rebuilding a coherent message. A separate piece flags the rise of right-leaning podcasters as a media force Democrats have failed to counter effectively. The picture is of a party with real electoral opportunities but unresolved questions about strategy and identity.

sources.title: Democrats have too many troubles to cheer Trump’s failures
Democrats announce first slate of state legislative targets ahead of November
Democrats face new reality: Podcasters’ political power
Democrats still have the edge heading into the Midterms

us-politics.headline: AI Rules, Tesla Probe, and California Rail Costs Dominate Tech and Policy
us-politics.body:
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman released a policy blueprint calling for federal taxes and regulations on artificial intelligence, a notable move from a leading industry figure. The proposal puts AI governance back on the legislative agenda at a moment when Congress has struggled to act.

Elsewhere, federal regulators closed their investigation into Tesla's 'Actually Smart Summon' feature, finding no grounds to proceed. And California's high-speed rail project now carries an estimated price tag of $126 billion, with no tracks yet laid - a figure that drew sharp criticism from fiscal conservatives and renewed questions about the project's viability.

sources.title: OpenAI’s Altman releases blueprint for taxing, regulating artificial intelligence
Regulator closes investigation into Tesla ‘actually smart summon’ feature
Newsom's California rail project now expected to cost $126B, official admits, with still no tracks laid

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Bites Tourism and Commuters Across Australia
au-politics.body:
Soaring fuel prices are reshaping daily life and regional economies. Sydney commuters are abandoning cars for bikes in growing numbers, while an outback pub in remote Queensland recorded just two visitors over Easter - a stark sign of how cost pressures are keeping travellers home.

The shift is not just inconvenient. Remote communities that depend on tourist traffic face real economic strain, and small operators have little buffer against prolonged downturns. The fuel crisis is quietly redrawing where Australians go and how they get there.

sources.title: Cars make way for bikes as Sydney commuters saddle up to circumvent ‘crazy’ fuel costs
Outback tourist pub had two visitors this Easter amid fuel price pinch

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila Threatens Far North Queensland Again
au-politics.body:
A second severe tropical cyclone in three weeks is bearing down on far north Queensland. Cyclone Maila is tracking toward the same region battered by Cyclone Narelle in mid-March, raising serious concerns about compounding damage to infrastructure and communities still recovering.

Emergency services and residents face the grim prospect of responding to a major weather event before the last one is fully dealt with. The back-to-back cyclones will test the capacity of state and federal disaster response systems and renew debate about climate resilience in northern Australia.

sources.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila could hit far north Queensland three weeks after Narelle tore through

au-politics.headline: AI in Government Decision-Making Draws Robodebt Comparisons
au-politics.body:
Scientists are warning that using artificial intelligence to prepare and evaluate environmental impact assessments risks repeating the failures of the robodebt scheme. Their concern centres on AI systems producing outputs that appear authoritative but contain errors that go unchecked - with potentially serious consequences for ecosystems and affected communities.

The Spectator, from the right, separately criticises the government's rollout of free online mental health services as emblematic of bureaucratic overreach. Both critiques, from opposite ends of the spectrum, point to a shared anxiety: that automated or remote government services can cause harm when accountability is weak.

sources.title: Using AI to prepare and evaluate environmental assessments risks ‘robodebt-style’ failures, scientists say
Free online therapy from a headache-inducing government

au-politics.headline: Timor-Leste Linked to Alleged Sanctioned Scam Network
au-politics.body:
An investigation has revealed alleged links between Timor-Leste and a sanctioned criminal empire accused of running large-scale online scam operations. The report points to private jet travel, undeveloped resort land and remote coastal sites as part of a network with ties to the Prince Group, a conglomerate already subject to international sanctions.

The findings raise questions about oversight in Australia's near region and the role of foreign investment in Pacific and Southeast Asian nations with limited regulatory capacity. Australia has strategic and development interests in Timor-Leste, and the allegations add pressure on policymakers to scrutinise financial flows in the region more closely.

sources.title: Private jets, deserted shores and an unbuilt resort: alleged links to sanctioned ‘scam’ empire revealed in Timor-Leste

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Ceasefire Talks Stall
world.body:
Trump has threatened to strike Iranian power plants, pharmaceutical facilities, and civilian bridges, raising fears of a humanitarian catastrophe inside Iran. Pakistan has proposed a two-phase truce deal, but Iran has rejected any temporary ceasefire that does not lift the threat permanently, and has warned it could close the Bab al-Mandeb strait - a chokepoint carrying a significant share of global trade.

Trump also confirmed the US armed Iranian dissidents through Kurdish intermediaries, a claim Kurdish groups deny. A separate US special operations mission successfully extracted an American air officer from inside Iran. Yemen's Houthis have entered the conflict, deepening regional spillover and alarming Yemeni civilians already living under years of war.

sources.title: Where are Iran’s power plants that Trump has threatened to destroy?
In maps and photos: Five Iranian civilian bridges at risk of US strikes
Bombing of Iran's pharma producers threatens patients' lives
How US operation to rescue air officer from Iran unfolded
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Has Trump confirmed Iran’s claim that protesters were US-armed?
Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure: How would that affect world trade?
Pakistan offers ‘two-phased’ truce deal to end US-Israel war on Iran
Trump’s ‘Bridge Day’ threat: Can a last-ditch ceasefire plan work?
Yemen’s civilians fear fallout after Houthis enter Iran war
Trump says US armed Iranian dissidents via Kurds, Kurdish groups deny claim

world.headline: Israel Expands Strikes Across Gaza and Lebanon
world.body:
Israeli forces have intensified attacks on southern Lebanon, destroying entire towns and hitting areas previously designated as safe zones near Beirut. Up to 1.2 million people have been displaced, with families sheltering in mountain communities as their homes are reduced to rubble.

In Gaza, the economic collapse is accelerating. Young Palestinians face mass unemployment as Israel maintains a blockade that has effectively strangled the territory's economy. A grandmother who lost her entire family to Israeli strikes has become one of many faces of a civilian toll that shows no sign of easing.

sources.title: Gaza grandmother loses her family due to Israeli attacks
Jobless young Palestinians trapped as Israel holds Gaza’s economy hostage
Israel destroys southern Lebanon towns, hits ‘safe’ areas around Beirut
Up to 1.2 million people forced to flee as Israel pummels Lebanon
Families fleeing Israeli attacks take refuge in Lebanon’s mountains

world.headline: Ukraine Hits Russian Energy Infrastructure Deep Inside Russia
world.body:
Ukraine struck the Black Sea port city of Novorossiysk, a major Russian energy hub, and continued a campaign targeting oil refineries that has brought the smell of burning fuel to St Petersburg. The strikes mark a significant expansion of Ukraine's reach into Russian territory, hitting infrastructure central to Russia's export economy.

The attacks signal a deliberate Ukrainian strategy to pressure Russia's energy revenues rather than focus solely on front-line positions.

sources.title: Ukraine strikes Russian Black Sea energy hub Novorossiysk
‘Smell’ of war comes to St Petersburg as Ukraine hammers Russian refineries

world.headline: Myanmar Junta Chief Takes Presidency; Bangladesh Measles Crisis Grows
world.body:
Myanmar's military chief has been formally elected president, consolidating the junta's grip on power years after the 2021 coup. Analysts see little prospect of a democratic transition, with the move likely to deepen international isolation.

Separately, Bangladesh is battling a measles outbreak that has killed around 100 children in a single month. Authorities have launched emergency vaccination drives, but health workers warn that weakened immunisation coverage during recent political unrest left large numbers of children exposed.

sources.title: Myanmar junta chief elected president — what comes next?
Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh

ai.headline: OpenAI Sketches Economic Blueprint as Geopolitical Threats Mount
ai.body:
OpenAI published a policy vision calling for public wealth funds, taxes on robots, and a four-day work week to manage AI-driven economic disruption. The document signals the company is positioning itself as a stakeholder in post-automation policy, not just a technology vendor. It is a notable shift for a firm whose products are accelerating the very job displacement it now proposes to cushion.

Meanwhile, Iran has threatened OpenAI's Stargate data center under construction in Abu Dhabi, adding a hard security dimension to the company's Gulf expansion. The threat underscores how AI infrastructure is becoming a geopolitical target, and how quickly the ambitions of the Stargate project have drawn adversarial attention.

sources.title: OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi

ai.headline: AI Floods Music Charts and Rewires How Sellers Work
ai.body:
An AI-generated artist named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart, a concrete demonstration of how synthetic content can dominate commercial platforms without any human performer behind it. The story sits alongside a broader pattern: AI propaganda is increasingly engineered for virality, exploiting the same algorithmic incentives that once rewarded human creators.

Small online sellers are also reshaping their businesses around AI tools, using platforms like Alibaba's Accio to decide what products to make before committing to inventory. ChatGPT meanwhile launched app integrations with DoorDash, Spotify, and Uber, embedding the model deeper into everyday commercial transactions. Across music, retail, and services, AI is quietly becoming the layer between intent and action.

sources.title: AI Singer Now Occupies Eleven Spots on iTunes Singles Chart
When Virality Is the Message: The New Age of AI Propaganda
How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make

ai.headline: Intel Bets on Chip Packaging as AI Hardware Race Intensifies
ai.body:
Intel is wagering that advanced chip packaging - stacking and connecting multiple dies into a single module - could be its path back to relevance in the AI hardware boom. The approach sidesteps the raw transistor race and instead competes on how efficiently chips can be assembled and interconnected. Analysts suggest the bet could generate billions if AI demand for dense, efficient compute keeps climbing.

Cisco's CEO Chuck Robbins separately floated the idea of data centers in space, framing orbital infrastructure as a long-term answer to power and land constraints on the ground. Spain's Xoople also raised $130 million to build AI-ready geospatial mapping of the Earth. The infrastructure layer of AI is expanding in every direction.

sources.title: The Ridiculously Nerdy Intel Bet That Could Rake in Billions
Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins wants data centers in space
Spain’s Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI

ai.headline: Germany Names REvil Leader as Employers Mine Worker Data
ai.body:
German authorities publicly identified the operator behind REvil and GandCrab, two of the most destructive ransomware operations of the past decade. The doxing of the figure known as UNKN is a rare instance of Western law enforcement naming a Russian cybercriminal still believed to be operating freely inside Russia. It signals a shift toward public attribution as a deterrence tool.

On a quieter but equally invasive front, employers are increasingly using personal data scraped from outside sources to calculate the lowest salary a candidate will accept before making an offer. The practice turns job seekers' own digital footprints against them. Both stories point to the same underlying reality: data is now a weapon, and most people have no idea how it is being used.

sources.title: Germany Doxes "UNKN," Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
Employers use your personal data to figure out the lowest salary you'll accept

btc.headline: Bitcoin Reclaims $69K on Ceasefire Hopes, Shorts Squeezed
btc.body:
Bitcoin surged back above $69,000 Monday after reports of Iran ceasefire talks triggered a short squeeze, wiping out bearish positions that had built up during last week's selloff. Algorand and several altcoins extended gains alongside BTC, with NEAR Protocol up 8.1% over the weekend per the CoinDesk 20 index.

Analysts see room for further upside but flag macro headwinds - inflation data dominates the economic calendar this week and could cap any rally. Prediction markets are now functioning as real-time geopolitical sensors, with Iran war contracts on platforms like Kalshi moving in lockstep with broader risk assets, according to Sygnum.

sources.title: Bitcoin rallies on report of Iran ceasefire talks, Algorand extends gains
Bitcoin reclaims $69,000 as ceasefire talks surface and crypto shorts get squeezed
First real bull signal since 2025? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
Bitcoin has room to rally, but there's a catch
Inflation takes center stage: Crypto Week Ahead
Iran war bets turn prediction markets into real-time macro radar: Sygnum
CoinDesk 20 performance update: NEAR Protocol (NEAR) jumps 8.1% over weekend

btc.headline: Strategy Buys More Bitcoin While Paper Losses Hit $14.5B
btc.body:
Strategy added 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million last week, pushing its total holdings to nearly 767,000 BTC. The purchase came despite the firm reporting over $14.5 billion in paper losses for Q1 - a figure that underscores how aggressively it has bought through the downturn.

Separately, Bitmine's ether treasury reached 4.8 million ETH as the company prepares to list on the NYSE, signaling that the corporate crypto treasury playbook is spreading beyond bitcoin. Both moves reflect a broader institutional bet that current prices represent a buying opportunity rather than a warning sign.

sources.title: Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC
Bitmine's ether treasury hits 4.8 million ETH as stock listing moves to NYSE

btc.headline: Tokenization Goes Mainstream - With Warnings Attached
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon told JPMorgan staff the bank must accelerate its tokenization efforts as the technology begins reshaping settlement, lending, and asset management across traditional finance. His comments landed the same day the IMF cautioned that tokenization could import crypto's volatility and liquidity risks directly into global financial markets.

China's tax authority separately urged domestic banks to adopt blockchain for lending services - a rare endorsement of the technology from Beijing. Circle added another dimension, unveiling a quantum-resistant roadmap for its new Arc layer-1 blockchain, positioning the infrastructure ahead of cryptographic threats that most networks have yet to address.

sources.title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance
IMF warns tokenization could bring crypto risks into global financial markets
China's tax authority urges banks to implement blockchain for lending services
Circle future-proofs Arc blockchain against quantum computing threats
Circle unveils quantum-resistant roadmap for its layer-1 blockchain Arc

btc.headline: AI Deepfakes Crack Crypto KYC; North Korea Ran DeFi Ops for Years
btc.body:
A new AI-powered cybercrime toolkit is targeting crypto exchanges and banks by generating deepfakes capable of bypassing KYC verification systems - raising fresh questions about identity security across the industry. The tool is already circulating on criminal forums, researchers warn.

The threat compounds a longer-running problem: North Korean operatives have quietly embedded themselves inside DeFi protocols since at least 2019, according to a cybersecurity analyst who tracked the infiltration across dozens of projects. China also ordered Apple to remove Jack Dorsey's encrypted messaging app Bitchat from its App Store - the app had gained traction among Iranian protesters - highlighting how decentralized communication tools are drawing state-level pressure.

sources.title: New AI cybercrime tool targets crypto, bank KYC systems via deepfakes
North Korean workers have been infiltrating DeFi for 7 years: Researcher
China orders Apple to pull Dorsey's Bitchat, the messaging app used during Iran protests
Apple pulls Jack Dorsey's Bitchat from China at Beijing’s request

food.name: Miso-Glazed Cabbage Steaks
food.description: Thick-cut cabbage roasted until caramelised and lacquered with a sweet-salty miso and mirin glaze
food.tip: Score the cut face in a crosshatch before glazing so the miso penetrates rather than just sitting on the surface

exercise.name: Monday Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or dumbbells, a clear corridor or outdoor path
exercise.description: Alternate between farmer carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries for timed sets, building grip strength, core stability, and real-world functional fitness in a way machines never replicate

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had every component replaced remains fundamentally the same thing — and philosophers from Locke to Parfit have weaponised it to interrogate whether you are the same person you were ten years ago
learn.why: On a Monday at the start of a new week it is a quietly destabilising and productive question: if your cells, beliefs, and memories are all in flux, what exactly is making your plans for the week ahead?
